texto: I follow Plato only with my mindPure beauty strikes me as a little thinA little cold, however beautiful.I am in love with what is mixed and impureDoubtful, dark and hard to disencumberI want beauty I must dig for, search for.Pure beauty is beginning and not endBegin with the sun and drop from sun to cloudFrom cloud to tree, and from tree to earth itselfAnd deeper yet to the earth dark root.I am in love with what resists my lovingWith what I have to labor to make live.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Robert Francis sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “I follow Plato only with my mindPure beauty strikes me as a little thinA little cold, however bea…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, beautiful, beauty, útil em momentos de termino e reflexao-noturna.
